text:
RT @PolyReward: The only AI benchmark I care about:

can the agent farm Polymarket Rewards without nuking your wallet?

Right now, Codex an…
my card looks a bit cringe😂 https://t.co/1iOCGrFhuG

This guy made $7.5M on Polymarket 
by betting on a Czechia vs South Africa draw

After kickoff, it looked like Czechia had it — and his position crashed by over 50%.

Most people would’ve been cooked.

But South Africa somehow saved the draw… and GRIMDRIP cashed out $7.5M

His chart is really interesting

It looks like something changed big on Polymarket around early May 2026. Maybe the rewards structure got updated

Maker rebate also showed up and basically offset the new fees

So this account is a pretty clear example that the setup is still good for market makers

Fees do not seem to have hit them that hard

Can someone explain this?

@Eltonma has a crazy lead
Almost 3x ahead of the nearest competitors.

Most Polymarket rewards are from sports liquidity, right? But he’s not really a sports liquidity guy

And btw he’s probably taking the #1 spot soon https://t.co/32RMobOY9u
Crypto is completely fucked, so Jeff Bezos decided to support YOU personally🫵 https://t.co/akPOfoKdLt
Looks like Polymarket 2FA is coming very soon

I’ll be honest, I created  https://t.co/ITuRdYH450 @polytweetdotcom as an experimental project and I didn’t expect it to become this popular.
I didn’t even think it would get 1000 users

Keeping the project running requires a large number of API requests, which costs real money. The project has no monetization, so I reached out to @PolymarketDevs  and @williamlegate  asking if they could support the project if it’s useful for Polymarket, but no one replied

So unfortunately, I won’t be able to update it as often as I’d like

New Polymarket market idea:

Which happens first? AGI or @ESYudkowsky  getting the $POLY airdrop

The funniest part is, he’s actually an OG😅

Another funny detail:

Roman Yampolskiy @romanyam   talks about Polymarket in almost every podcast, but hasn’t posted a single tweet about it

I think he’s going to feel brutal FOMO if he doesn’t do it before the snapshot
Actually, the OGs know him for his famous Polypoll https://t.co/d5Hn6A6lWc

One user is posting complete nonsense and an AI bot is adding an incorrect link

What a mess https://t.co/7MqxHGKQ4P
still can’t believe https://t.co/VQWBwxl1TE was just sitting there for $14🤯

Why rewarding Twitter creators with $POLY would be genius marketing for Polymarket

Let’s be honest. Airdrops were never really just free money

They always had a purpose.

Turn the airdrop into marketing. Grow the user base.
For some projects, it was just a way to hide from the SEC😉

But in 2026, I think we can safely say the classic airdrop model is basically dead

The last airdrop I remember that actually felt somewhat meaningful for regular real users was Starknet

And to be fair, they did distribute a serious amount of money. A lot of people in the community were happy. I was one of the people who got the Starknet airdrop too, so thank you

But if we are talking about efficiency, did it help Starknet become popular? Did it help them build a real, active, living community around the project?

Absolutely not

You can almost say that money was wasted.

Now look at Polymarket

From an efficiency point of view, giving a meaningful part of the airdrop to the people who have been talking about Polymarket for months and in some cases for years, would be one of the smartest marketing decisions they could make

Because the moment you reward these people, you don’t just "pay users"

You increase their loyalty. 

You give them even more motivation to talk about the project

You turn the people who already carried the narrative into even stronger believers

And unlike a typical crypto airdrop, where thousands of sybil wallets can belong to one person who will never write anything except “farmed another project, thanks for the money,” here you have a real anti-sybil metric.

People literally passed Proof of Humanity day after day, year after year

They posted. They replied. They argued.

This is not some random wallet activity
This is actual human effort

Twitter became the reputation layer

And Polymarket creators already proved they are real humans in the most public way possible

Honestly, this might be the most genius anti-sybil filter of all time
Holy shit https://t.co/tUx2crBAQH

Polymarket has the most active community in crypto right now

Just a fact.

I vibe-coded a leaderboard, but the idea is that to appear on it, users have to come to the site themselves and enter their own nickname

While I was asleep, more than 2,500 people did that!

The most active community in the world

Thank you to everyone who sent words of support and suggestions on how to improve the site!
———————
Just a reminder: Twitter API limitations don’t allow me to scan more than 3,200 tweets/replies

But that also means the Polymarket team probably wouldn’t have that ability either, so everyone is being ranked under the same real-world conditions

If even Nikita Bier made 4 posts about Polymarket
you have absolutely no excuses https://t.co/DJasrVo2P1

I’m literally begging someone to check this bro’s post!

Sooo Early https://t.co/icYoOv7tLV

We will scan all of Twitter! 
We will find everyone who has ever posted about Polymarket! 
No one will go unnoticed https://t.co/1BGol3K93o

Btw If you found this dashboard useful, follow @polytweetdotcom 

I promise I won’t spam you with AI slop or ads. I just don’t want the page to look dead
Thanks

An OG is someone who wrote their first tweet about Polymarket before the 2024 U.S. presidential election

I think Polymarket only became truly mainstream after that
